(Singapore)</p></td><td><p><code>ap-southeast</code></p></td><td><p><code>myapp.sgp-s1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td><td><p><code>&lt;app&gt;.internal-&lt;space-id&gt;.sgp-s1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Australia (Sydney)</p></td><td><p><code>ap-southeast-2</code></p></td><td><p><code>myapp.aus-s1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td><td><p><code>&lt;app&gt;.internal-&lt;space-id&gt;.aus-s1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Japan (Tokyo)</p></td><td><p><code>ap-northeast</code></p></td><td><p><code>myapp.jpn-e1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td><td><p><code>&lt;app&gt;.internal-&lt;space-id&gt;.jpn-e1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><p>Europe</p></td><td><p>Ireland (Dublin)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-west</code></p></td><td><p><code>myapp.irl-e1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td><td><p><code>&lt;app&gt;.internal-&lt;space-id&gt;.irl-e1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Germany (Frankfurt)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-central</code></p></td><td><p><code>myapp.deu-c1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td><td><p><code>&lt;app&gt;.internal-&lt;space-id&gt;.deu-c1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>UK (London)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-west-2</code></p></td><td><p><code>myapp.gbr-e1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td><td><p><code>&lt;app&gt;.internal-&lt;space-id&gt;.gbr-e1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p><a href="https://eu1.anypoint.mulesoft.com">EU Cloud</a></p></td><td rowspan="2"><p>Europe</p></td><td><p>Ireland (Dublin)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-west</code></p></td><td><p><code>myapp.irl-e1.eu1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Germany (Frankfurt)</p></td><td><p><code>eu-central</code></p></td><td><p><code>myapp.deu-c1.eu1.cloudhub.io</code></p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><a href="https://gov.anypoint.mulesoft.com">MuleSoft Government Cloud</a></p></td><td><p>US Government</p></td><td><p>US GovCloud (West)</p></td><td><p><code>us-gov-west</code></p></td><td><p><code>myapp.usg-w1.gov.cloudhub.io</code></p></td><td><p>-</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] URL formats vary by control plane:
>
> US Cloud Control Plane
>
> URLs donât include a region suffix before `cloudhub.io`.  
> Example: `myapp.usa-e1.cloudhub.io`
>
> Non-US Control Planes
>
> URLs include a region suffix (such as `eu1`, `ca1`, or `jp1`) before `cloudhub.io`.  
> Examples: `myapp.irl-e1.eu1.cloudhub.io`, `myapp.can-c1.ca1.cloudhub.io`,
> `myapp.jpn-e1.jp1.cloudhub.io`

## See Also

- [Private Spaces](ch2-private-space-about)
- [Configure SSL Endpoints and Certificates](ps-config-ssl-endpoints)
---

# CloudHub 2.0 Operating System Patch Updates

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Because CloudHub 2.0 is an integration platform as a service (iPaaS), it manages operating system
patching for you, enabling you to focus on developing and updating your applications.

CloudHub 2.0 applies security patches as needed to ensure that your application is secure.

Patch updates use blue-green deployment for applications that use the Rolling Update deployment
model, which ensures zero downtime for applications that support it.

CloudHub 2.0 applies continuous updates to two MuleSoft-managed deployment platforms:

- US cloud
- EU cloud

## CloudHub 2.0 Infrastructure Updates

As a container-based platform, CloudHub 2.0 applies infrastructure fixes and security patches as
needed to ensure the infrastructure is secure and up-to-date. Patch updates use rolling upgrade
deployment (when selected as the deployment model) and can include operating system changes or
changes to internal software. An update rotates, drains, and replaces the underlying hosts gradually
to ensure zero downtime when using the Rolling Update deployment model. During the upgrade, the Mule
application might be restarted onto a new secure host. If the Mule worker is not running with
multiple replicas or is using the Recreate deployment model, it experiences slight downtime.

When an update is available, CloudHub 2.0 includes it in Production environments as the default for
new private spaces. The existing infrastructure patching schedule occurs in the same maintenance
window as
[CloudHub 2.0 Runtime patches](../release-notes/cloudhub-2/cloudhub-2-runtimes-release-notes).
Infrastructure and runtime updates might cause your applications to restart.

[Anypoint VPNs](../cloudhub/vpn-maintenance) used in CloudHub 2.0 are upgraded continuously.
Whenever a new update is available, the VPN is patched within the service-level agreement. If the
Anypoint VPN is not set up as high availability or if the non-high-availability VPN is not set up
with two tunnels, downtime is expected.

### Clustered Replicas and Rolling Updates

When running Mule in a [cluster](ch2-clustering), a rolling update with a Mule runtime version that
has a Hazelcast version change can result in multiple members of the cluster with different
Hazelcast versions. This might cause application failure, with a Hazelcast serialization error, if
the different Hazelcast versions are incompatible.

To prevent this kind of error, use the Recreate update strategy.

## Schedule Infrastructure Upgrades (Optional)

Schedule infrastructure upgrades earlier than the scheduled patching process, between the Release
Available and the Auto-Update dates stated in the
[monthly updates table](../release-notes/cloudhub-2/cloudhub-2-runtimes-release-notes). This way,
you can choose a date in the future that better suits your organizationâs needs.

On average, this upgrade takes between thirty to sixty minutes to complete. If your upgrade is
queued, it can take longer than thirty minutes. The impact of the upgrade process on running
applications is consistent with the auto-patching behavior described in the previous sections.

You can modify or delete your scheduled upgrade if it is not already in progress, as indicated by
the `Queued` status. If you cancel the schedule and do not create another or opt in to an earlier
upgrade, the upgrade occurs during the mandatory patching window.

### Schedule the Upgrade via Runtime Manager

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager** > **Private Spaces**.

    If an upgrade is available for a private space, the **Upgrade Available** status appears under
    the **Infra Status** column.

2.  Click **Schedule an Upgrade**.
3.  Select the date on which you want your private space to upgrade.  
    Upgrades occur at 00:00 in your local timezone on the date you select.
4.  Select the checkbox to confirm that you agree to upgrade early.
5.  Click **Upgrade**.

After applying the upgrade, you can check the status under the **Infra Status** column.

## Opt-in Early Infrastructure Upgrades (Optional)

Opt in to apply infrastructure upgrades earlier than the scheduled patching process, between the
Release Available and the Auto-Update dates stated in the
[monthly updates table](../release-notes/cloudhub-2/cloudhub-2-runtimes-release-notes). This way,
you can choose the time that better suits your organizationâs needs.

On average, this upgrade takes between thirty to sixty minutes to complete. If your upgrade is
queued, it can take longer than thirty minutes. The impact of the upgrade process on running
applications is consistent with the auto-patching behavior described in the previous sections.

> [!NOTE] This upgrade is irreversible. Ensure that you understand the impact of the upgrade and
> choose when to trigger the upgrade based on your organizationâs needs before applying it in a
> production environment.

### Apply the Opt-in Upgrade via Runtime Manager

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager** > **Private Spaces**.

    If an upgrade is available for a private space, the **Upgrade Available** status appears under
    the **Infra Status** column.

2.  Click **Upgrade available**.
3.  Click **Upgrade now** and opt in to the early upgrade.
4.  Click **Upgrade**.

After applying the upgrade, you can check the status under the **Infra Status** column.

### Schedule or Apply the Upgrade via API

You can upgrade a private space using the Upgrade Private Space API. For more information, see the
[Upgrade Private Space API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/cloudhub-20-api/minor/1.0/console/endpoint/%232428/)
documentation in Exchange.

## Monthly Date-Patch Updates

In the first full week of each month, MuleSoft releases a _date-patch update_, which includes
backward-compatible bug fixes to address any issues discovered in Mule runtime engine. The monthly
date-patch update might include security patches if the scheduled date is within the security SLA.

The monthly patches update applications only to the latest date patch for the patch version. They do
not change the patch version number.

The format of the version number for these updates to 4.3.x or 4.4.x versions is:

_Major_._Minor_._Patch_ : _Date_

Hereâs an example version number, including the patch date:

`4.4.0:20211015`

Starting with Mule 4.5, MuleSoft introduces two new release channels, Edge and Long-term Support
(LTS). The Mule runtime versioning schema for the new release channels is:

_Major\[numeric\] . Minor\[numeric\] . Patch\[numeric\] : Build\[numeric\] Channel\[e for edge,
nothing for LTS\]_

Some examples of these values are: `4.5.0:1e` for Edge, and `4.6.0:1` for LTS.

### Update Schedule

- In the first full week of the month, MuleSoft releases the date patch.
- In the third full week of the month, MuleSoft automatically applies the date patch that was
  released during the first week of the month.
  - Applications in environments other than production are updated during the week.
  - Production applications are updated on the weekend.

When an automatic update occurs, the audit log for your application adds an entry by user Anypoint
Staff, which shows the date and time an update occurred and whether it was successful. If the update
is successful, no action is required. If your application does not support zero-downtime updates or
requires a manual update, you can manually apply the date patch released in the first full week of
the month in week 1 or week 2. See
[Update the Runtime Version Manually](#update-the-runtime-version-manually).

## Edge and LTS Releases for Mule

Starting with Mule 4.5, MuleSoft introduces two new release channels, Edge and Long-Term Support
(LTS). Both release channels are available in all our deployment models: Anypoint Runtime Fabric,
CloudHub, CloudHub 2.0, and Hybrid Standalone.

Release cadence refers to new Mule runtime versions. Patch updates for supported versions continue
to follow the monthly patch update schedule.

Consider these factors to determine the approach that best suits your requirements ahead of the Mule
4.4 End of Standard Support.

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Channel Type</th><th>Release Cadence</th><th>Differentiators</th><th>Support Coverage</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p>Edge</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Up to three times per year</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Includes new features on a frequent release cadence with shorter maintenance coverage.</p></li><li><p>Versions arenât FedRAMP approved.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For versions 4.9 and later:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Standard Support until one month after the next Edge version is released</p></li><li><p>3 months of Extended Support</p></li></ul></div><div><p>For version 4.8:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>5 months of Standard Support</p></li><li><p>3 months of Extended Support</p></li></ul></div><div><p>For versions 4.5 to 4.7:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>4 months of Standard Support</p></li><li><p>4 months of Extended Support</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p>LTS</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Periodic (Scheduled for February)</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p>Includes new features introduced in prior Edge releases, along with new features introduced in the February Edge release. This release is maintained for an extended period over being the first to adopt new capabilities.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>For version 4.6 and later:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>A minimum of 18 months of Standard Support</p></li><li><p>A minimum of 6 months of Extended Support</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

If an LTS version is released in February, both Edge and LTS share the same underlying version,
including the same minor Mule runtime version and new features.

To review the new support policy that aligns with the new release periods and types of releases, see
[MuleSoft Support Policy](https://www.salesforce.com/en-us/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/documents/legal/Agreements/versioning-back-support-policy.pdf).

> [!NOTE] For information about the monthly patch update process, see
> [Update Patches to Mule](../release-notes/mule-runtime/patching-mule-versions).

## Edge Channel

Starting with Mule 4.5, MuleSoft releases Edge versions up to three times a year. Use the Edge
channel if you want to stay on top of the latest features and feel comfortable with a more frequent
release cycle and auto-app upgrade cycle. The Edge channel includes the latest features and
innovations available on the MuleSoft platform.

## LTS Channel

Starting with Mule 4.6, MuleSoft releases Long-Term Support (LTS) versions periodically, scheduled
for February. If youâre on Mule 4.4, you arenât required to upgrade to Mule 4.6. However, because
Mule 4.5 was released in October 2023, Standard Support for Mule 4.4 ends in October 2024, followed
by the end of Extended Support in October 2025.

For CloudHub and CloudHub 2.0 customers, once Standard Support for Mule 4.4 ends, if you want to
deploy new apps, you must do so on Mule 4.6 LTS or Mule 4.8 Edge.

Use the LTS channel if you want to avoid maintaining new versions frequently. The LTS channel adopts
new capabilities after they have been introduced via Edge releases.

## Switch from an Edge Version to an LTS Version

You can switch from an Edge version to an LTS version and vice versa. However, when moving from Edge
to LTS, you must choose a later LTS version. For example, if you choose Mule 4.6 LTS and you want to
implement a specific new feature released on Mule 4.7 Edge, you can opt into that Edge release, then
merge into Mule 4.9 LTS once it is available. MuleSoft does not support apps that are switched from
Edge to an older LTS version. For example, switching an app from Mule 4.7 Edge to Mule 4.6 LTS might
result in losing some of the new features in the older LTS version.

## Mule Runtime Release Cadence Support

The following table shows the Mule runtime release cadence support for CloudHub 2.0:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Mule Version</th><th>Release Date</th><th>Java Version</th><th>End of Standard Support</th><th>End of Extended Support</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>4.12 Edge</p></td><td><p>June 2026</p></td><td><p>17</p></td><td><p>November 2026</p></td><td><p>February 2027</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.11 Edge</p></td><td><p>February 2026</p></td><td><p>17</p></td><td><p>July 2026</p></td><td><p>October 2026</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.10 Edge</p></td><td><p>October 2025</p></td><td><p>17</p></td><td><p>March 2026</p></td><td><p>June 2026</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.9 LTS</p></td><td><p>February 2025</p></td><td><p>17</p></td><td><p>August 2027</p></td><td><p>February 2028</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.9 Edge</p></td><td><p>February 2025</p></td><td><p>17</p></td><td><p>November 2025</p></td><td><p>February 2026</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.8 Edge</p></td><td><p>October 2024</p></td><td><p>8, 11, and 17</p></td><td><p>March 2025</p></td><td><p>June 2025</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.7 Edge</p></td><td><p>June 2024</p></td><td><p>8, 11, and 17</p></td><td><p>October 2024</p></td><td><p>February 2025</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.6 LTS</p></td><td><p>February 2024</p></td><td><p>8, 11, and 17</p></td><td><p>August 2026</p></td><td><p>February 2027</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.6 Edge</p></td><td><p>February 2024</p></td><td><p>8, 11, and 17</p></td><td><p>June 2024</p></td><td><p>October 2024</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4.5 Edge</p></td><td><p>October 2023</p></td><td><p>8 and 11</p></td><td><p>February 2024</p></td><td><p>June 2024</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

> [!NOTE] MuleSoft provides an extended support window for version 4.6 LTS due to its compatibility
> with Java 8, 11, and 17.

> [!NOTE] If you are a CloudHub or a CloudHub 2.0 customer and you want to create or test Mule 4.6
> applications in Anypoint Studio, the Studio version required is the next upcoming release in
> March. Meanwhile, MuleSoft enables you to deploy new apps in Mule runtime 4.5e version via API,
> Mule Maven plugin, and Anypoint Platform CLI starting February 14 until April 19. Auto-upgrade
> from Mule 4.5 Edge to Mule 4.6 Edge for CloudHub and CloudHub 2.0 applications will be performed
> in April instead of February. See
> [Temporary changes to auto-upgrade and new app deployment for Mule 4.5 Edge](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=001117251&type=1).

## Deployment Models and Application Lifecycle Management

Application lifecycle management depends on the deployment model.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>CloudHub 2.0</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Deploy net new apps</p></td><td><p>Deploy to the latest minor-patch version available under Standard Support of each channel.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Auto-upgrade</p></td><td><div><div><p>Patches: Monthly auto-patching upgrade when a new patch version is available, as long as the appâs TLS version is compatible with the new patch version. See patching schedule for <a href="../release-notes/cloudhub/cloudhub-runtimes-release-notes">CloudHub</a> and <a href="../release-notes/cloudhub-2/cloudhub-2-runtimes-release-notes">CloudHub 2.0</a>.</p></div><div><p>Runtime version compatibility: Apps running Mule runtime 4.9 or earlier arenât auto-upgraded to Mule runtime 4.10 or later. Mule 4.10 removes support for TLS 1.0 and 1.1, which can cause compatibility issues and break existing applications if upgraded automatically. Apps on earlier versions (for example, 4.6) are auto-upgraded to the latest available patch version in the 4.9 release line. To use Mule runtime 4.10 or later, upgrade your application manually.</p></div><div><p>Channels: All apps are auto-upgraded to the latest minor version within their respective channels (Edge or LTS) during the monthly patching schedule of the month when previous version goes out of Standard Support.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Edge: auto-upgrade occurs six weeks after the version reaches its End of Standard Support.</p></li><li><p>LTS: auto-upgrade occurs six weeks after the version reaches its End of Standard Support.</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Self-upgrade</p></td><td><div><div><p>The auto-upgrade takes place during a fixed patching schedule after the versionâs standard support ends. See patching schedule for <a href="../release-notes/cloudhub/cloudhub-runtimes-release-notes">CloudHub</a> and <a href="../release-notes/cloudhub-2/cloudhub-2-runtimes-release-notes">CloudHub 2.0</a>.</p></div><div><ul><li><p>Edge: six weeks</p></li><li><p>LTS: six months</p></li></ul></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p>Rollback</p></td><td><p>Available to the previously used version</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Restart apps</p></td><td><p>Always</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keep running applications</p></td><td><p>Until End of Extended Support</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>End of Extended Support</p></td><td><p>Shutdown running apps.<br>Starting with Mule 4.7, CloudHub and CloudHub 2.0 applications in the Edge or LTS channels that are still running <a href="https://www.salesforce.com/en-us/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/documents/legal/Agreements/versioning-back-support-policy.pdf">End of Life versions</a> will undergo a forced upgrade to the latest version during the monthly upgrade window (See upgrade window for <a href="../release-notes/cloudhub/cloudhub-runtimes-release-notes">CloudHub</a> and <a href="../release-notes/cloudhub-2/cloudhub-2-runtimes-release-notes">CloudHub 2.0</a>).</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Retirement</p></td><td><p>Mule apps donât reach End of Life because theyâre always auto-upgraded.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supportability</p></td><td><p>All Mule apps are under Standard Support.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Mule Runtime Version Naming Changes

The version naming convention depends on the deployment model youâre using. A version increments:

- MAJOR when a release includes features that introduce breaking changes and backward
  incompatibility.
- MINOR when a release includes all new features keeping backward compatibility with previous
  minors.
- PATCH when a release includes bug fixes and security updates that include upgrades to libraries
  with reported vulnerabilities. We build a new runtime and do a full validation test.
- BUILD when a release includes changes related to Image/AMI, including OS changes, OS security
  updates, and changes in products outside the runtime. It doesnât include any runtime changes.

### CloudHub 2.0

The Mule runtime versioning schema for the new release channels is:

_Major\[numeric\] . Minor\[numeric\] . Patch\[numeric\] : Build\[numeric\] Channel\[e for edge,
nothing for LTS\]_

Each February, MuleSoft releases both an Edge and an LTS release with the same _Major.Minor_
version. To distinguish the versions, theyâre represented as:

- Edge: 4.6.0:1e
- LTS: 4.6.0:1

CloudHub 2.0 requires regular OS updates, hence the addition of the build enumeration in the full
runtime version schema.

Here are examples of the version numbers:

- Edge: 4.5.0:1e
- Edge: 4.6.0:1e
- LTS: 4.6.0:1

Mule runtime does not introduce a semVer increment if there is a month with no fixes.

Here is an example of the versioning schema using different patch and builds:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th></th><th></th><th></th><th></th><th>Hybrid Standalone</th><th>CloudHub - AMI / CloudHub 2.0 / Runtime Fabric - Docker image</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><th><p>Case</p></th><th><p>Release Date</p></th><th><p>Description</p></th><th><p>Patch/Build</p></th><th><p>Mule Runtime</p></th><th><p>Runtime Manager/Maven/API Tag for New Deployment</p></th></tr><tr><td><p>1</p></td><td><p>Oct 3, 2023</p></td><td><p>First release of 4.5.0</p></td><td><p>0th patch and 1st build</p></td><td><p>4.5.0</p></td><td><p>4.5.0:1e</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>2</p></td><td><p>Nov 7, 2023</p></td><td><p>Second release of 4.5</p></td><td><p>1st patch and 1st build</p></td><td><p>4.5.1</p></td><td><p>4.5.1:1e</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>3</p></td><td><p>Nov 7, 2023</p></td><td><p>Another build on same day for CloudHub, CloudHub 2.0, and Runtime Fabric</p></td><td><p>1st patch and 2nd build</p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td><td><p>4.5.1:2e</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>4</p></td><td><p>Nov 7, 2023</p></td><td><p>OS updates only for CloudHub, CloudHub 2.0, and Runtime Fabric</p></td><td><p>1st patch and 3rd build</p></td><td><p>Not Available</p></td><td><p>4.5.1:3e</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>5</p></td><td><p>Dec 5, 2023</p></td><td><p>Second patch version of 4.5</p></td><td><p>2nd patch and 1st build</p></td><td><p>4.5.2</p></td><td><p>4.5.2:1e</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>6</p></td><td><p>Jan 2, 2024</p></td><td><p>Hot fix for a Mule runtime regression</p></td><td><p>3rd patch &amp; 1st build</p></td><td><p>4.5.3</p></td><td><p>4.5.3:1e</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>7</p></td><td><p>Feb 6, 2024</p></td><td><p>Release of new minor</p></td><td><p>0th patch and 1st build</p></td><td><p>4.6.0</p></td><td><p>4.6.0:1e (Edge) / 4.6.0:1 (LTS)</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

The Mule runtime versioning schema uses these conventions:

- Patch number in schema
  - In the schema `4.5.X:2e`, the patch number is the X.
  - The patch number starts from `0`, introducing a new minor version.
  - The patch number increments when the release introduces new code changes, including hotfixes to
    regressions or other bug fixes.

- Build number in schema
  - In the schema `4.5.1:Ye`, the build number is Y.
  - The build number starts from `1`, introducing the first AMI or Docker image build of the patch.
  - The build number increments whenever the release introduces a new build of the AMI or Docker
    image for the same Mule runtime version. This increment does not indicate code changes to Mule
    runtime.

## Update Notification

When a new runtime version is available:

- The **Applications** page displays an **Update Available** column that indicates if there is an
  update available for the application:

  ![ch2 update available column](_images/ch2-update-available-column.png)

- **Update available** and the new available updates appear in the **Deployment Target** tab of the
  **Settings** page for the application:

  ![ch2 update available field](_images/ch2-update-available-field.png)

MuleSoft applies the updates automatically, based on the published schedules. If you want, you can
update them manually at your convenience.

## Update the Runtime Version Manually

1.  Log in to Anypoint Platform.
2.  Navigate to Runtime Manager.
3.  In the Runtime Manager navigation menu, click **Applications**
4.  Select the application you want to update.
5.  In **Settings**, in the **Deployment Target** tab, click the **Runtime Version** drop-down.
6.  Select the latest build for your version.
7.  Click **Deploy**.  
    The application redeploys with the new runtime version.

If you select the Edge release channel, a banner appears indicating the date of the next automatic
update.

![Banner indicating the date of automatic update for Edge release channel.](_images/ch2-app-edge-auto-update.png)

## Possible Status Types for Private Space Upgrades and Updates

The **Infra Status** column in the **Private Space** page shows the status of infrastructure
upgrades for your private space.

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Status</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Latest</p></td><td><p>The private space is running the latest available infrastructure version. It is currently not undergoing an infrastructure upgrade or configuration update.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upgrade Available</p></td><td><p>An upgrade is available for the private space. You can either initiate an upgrade immediately or schedule a time for the upgrade to begin.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upgrade Scheduled</p></td><td><p>The private space has an upgrade scheduled for a future date.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Queued</p></td><td><p>The upgrade that was scheduled or initiated is about to begin. You can no longer make changes to the schedule or interrupt the upgrade.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upgrading</p></td><td><p>The private space is undergoing an infrastructure upgrade, either scheduled, self-triggered, or as part of a monthly infrastructure upgrade.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Updating</p></td><td><p>The private space is undergoing an update that was triggered by a configuration change.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Failed</p></td><td><p>The private space experienced a failure which can be related to a user configuration update, or to an infrastructure upgrade, either scheduled, self-triggered, or as part of a monthly infrastructure upgrade.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Degraded</p></td><td><p>The private space is in degraded state.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>âââ</p></td><td><p>Unable to get the details of the private space status and the status is unknown.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Security Updates

MuleSoft regularly runs scans to identify security vulnerabilities in JVM and the underlying
operating system, and then automatically applies security patches based on the following SLA:

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Severity Level</th><th>Severity Definition</th><th>Patch Applied Within</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>P0</p></td><td><p>Critical</p></td><td><p>7 days</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>P1</p></td><td><p>High</p></td><td><p>30 days</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Check Your Application Status After an Update

After a new update occurs, you can check your
[applications status state](ch2-update-apps#replica-statuses) in the **Status** column in Runtime
Managerâs **Applications** tab.

You can also check your application logs to verify if your application was deployed correctly and
take any remediation actions in the event of some auto-minor update-related issue. For more
information, visit [Viewing Log Data for Deployed Apps](ch2-view-logs).

## See Also

- [Edge and LTS Releases for Mule](../release-notes/mule-runtime/lts-edge-release-cadence).

# Run Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Tests

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

If your company security policies require that you perform vulnerability and penetration testing
against Anypoint Platform, you can commence testing without notifying Salesforce beforehand.

All assessments must be performed in accordance with the
[Security Assessment Agreement](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000392845&type=1).

## Run Penetration and Vulnerability Tests

Because penetration testing can interfere with other tenants, MuleSoft allows penetration testing on
your workers but not on other Anypoint Platform services.

> [!IMPORTANT] Salesforce does not address vulnerabilities found with custom development. You must
> validate and fix any findings with your custom development.

### Prerequisites

Before initiating penetration testing:

- Assign a security resource at your company to review and validate findings from the tests.

### Steps

1.  Run the penetration tests.

    The Security Assessment Agreement includes restrictions and requirements for testing.

2.  Have your security resource use the following documents to identify common false positives and
    security issues related to settings:
    - [Platform Security FAQ](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000383448&type=1)
    - [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com/s/)

3.  If your assessment generates vulnerability findings, complete validation for any automated
    finding. Send all outstanding validated security vulnerability findings to the Salesforce
    Security Team at [security.salesforce.com/contact](https://security.salesforce.com/contact).

    Include the following information in your email:
    - Confirmation number for your Security Assessment approved by Salesforce
    - Summary of all findings and the associated severity level of each finding
    - Detailed assessment report that notes each finding
    - Steps to reproduce the vulnerability
    - All applicable HTTP requests and responses
    - Explanation of why the example is considered a finding

### Reporting Security Vulnerabilities

To report security vulnerability findings, follow the
[Security Vulnerability Finding Submittal Guide](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000384043&type=1).
Security vulnerability reports that do not follow the required steps are rejected.

Salesforce does not address any security vulnerabilities in the following categories:

- Scan output from automated vulnerability scanning tools without a valid proof of concept
- Security bugs without valid proof of concept
- Vulnerabilities identified in old or deprecated versions of Mule runtime engine
- Vulnerabilities introduced via code customization made by your company
- Known security issues that donât pose any risk

## See Also

- [CloudHub 2.0 Overview](./)
- [Deploying Apps to CloudHub 2.0](ch2-deploy)
- [Managing Apps Deployed to CloudHub 2.0](ch2-manage-apps)
- [Deployment Options](../runtime-manager/deployment-strategies)
---

# Private Spaces

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

A _private space_ is a virtual, private, and isolated logical space in CloudHub 2.0 in which to run
your apps. You can create multiple private spaces, either in the same or different regions.

You connect your private intranet to your private space to function as a single, private network.

In each private space, you define:

- A _private network_, which is a virtual cloud where apps deployed to this private space run.
- One or more connections from the private network to your external network, either via Anypoint VPN
  or a transit gateway.
- TLS contexts, which define the domains that are available when deploying apps to the private
  spaces, and optionally enable mutual TLS.
- Firewall rules to allow and block inbound and outbound traffic to your private space.
- The environments and business groups to allow to deploy to the private space.

When you create a private space, your license for Mule runtime is automatically injected and managed
by MuleSoft.

## Private Network

When you create the private network, you associate a range of IP addresses for the apps in your
private space to use, the region in which they run, and optionally, any internal DNS servers to
resolve requests to custom domains.

You can configure multiple private spaces in a single region, enabling you to set up separate
isolated networks for your production and non-production environments, such as dev or staging.

For information about regions, see
[Private Network Region](ps-gather-setup-info#private-network-region).

## Private Network Connections

You can connect a private space to your private network using the following methods:

- [Anypoint Virtual Private Network](ps-vpn-about)
- [Transit Gateway Attachments](ps-tgw-about)

## TLS Contexts

Using Transport Layer Security (TLS) contexts, CloudHub 2.0 enables clients to use custom domains
and paths to reach apps deployed to private spaces.

When setting up a private space, you configure Transport Layer Security (TLS) contexts that define
the domains that are available when deploying apps to the private spaces, and optionally enable
mutual TLS.

When deploying an app to the private space, you can use the domains from the TLS contexts to
configure multiple endpoints, which clients use to reach the app from the internet. If the domains
in the TLS context include a wildcard, you can configure an optional subdomain, such as the app name
or an organization. You can create as many endpoints as you need.

## Path Rewriting

You can configure the app to be accessible from a different path from the base path where the app is
listening.

## Firewall Rules

You control the traffic to and from your private spaces.

Inbound

By default, CloudHub 2.0 allows traffic to the private space on ports 80 (HTTP) and 443 (HTTPS) from
anywhere (0.0.0.0/0). Configure firewall rules to allow connections through specified ports, remove
ports, or use ports 80 and 443 to control traffic to your private space.

The private space provides load balancing to balance inbound traffic to apps across multiple
replicas.

Outbound

By default, CloudHub 2.0 allows all traffic from the private space to destinations outside the
private space. Configure firewall rules to remove this rule and allow traffic only on specified
ports.

By combining firewall rules and TLS context configuration, you can fine-tune the ingress and egress
of your application traffic.

## See Also

- [Creating Private Spaces](ps-create-configure)
- [Creating VPN Connections](ps-create-configure-vpn)
- [Creating Transit Gateway Attachments](ps-create-configure-tgw)
- [Configuring Private Spaces](ps-configure)
- [Configuring Domains and Certificates (TLS Context) for a Private Space](ps-config-domains)
- [Configuring Firewall Rules to Manage Traffic to and from a Private Space](ps-config-fw-rules)
- [Associating Business Groups and Environments with a Private Space](ps-config-env)
- [Configuring Endpoints and Paths for Apps Deployed to a Private Space](ch2-config-endpoints-paths)
---

# Protecting App Property Values

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

CloudHub 2.0 enables you to protect application property values by displaying the property name but
not its value, in Anypoint Runtime Manager.

Additionally, the value is not viewable or retrievable by any user. CloudHub 2.0 resolves the
property internally at runtime. These protected application values are encrypted and stored in the
Anypoint Security secrets manager, which, in turn, is encrypted per user organization. Because the
values are encrypted, you cannot view them in Anypoint Security secrets manager or Anypoint Runtime
Manager.

> [!IMPORTANT] Protected property values are separate from the encrypted Mule application properties
> stored in secure configuration files. See
> [Secure Configuration Properties](../mule-runtime/latest/secure-configuration-properties).

## Protect Property Values in Runtime Manager

Set protected properties via the table view for granular control over each property and value.

Although you can attempt to protect a single property more than once, Runtime Manager issues a
warning and protects only the final instance of that property.

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager** > **Applications**.
2.  Click the app name.
3.  In the navigation menu, click **Settings**.
4.  On the **Settings** page, click the **Properties** tab.
5.  Depending on how many properties you want to protect, choose either:
    - To protect properties one at a time, click **Table view**.
    - To protect multiple properties, use **Text view** to add the key-value pairs, switch to
      **Table view**, and continue to Step 8 to protect the properties.

6.  In the **New Key** field, enter the property name to protect.
7.  In the **New Value** field, enter the property value.
8.  In the value field, click **Protect** and then click **Protect value** to confirm.

    ![Protect button on the Properties tab](_images/ch2-protected-props-protect.png)

9.  Depending on the deployment status, choose either:
    - If this application is deployed, click **Apply Changes**.
    - If youâre ready to deploy the application, click **Deploy Application**.

On the **Properties** tab, the values for properties that you just protected are now no longer
visible to you or any other user.

In the following example, the values for `birthdate` and `password` are protected, but the value for
`username` is not:

![Protected values on the Properties tab on the application Settings page](_images/ch2-protected-props.png)

After you commit the values and deploy the app, the protected property values donât appear in the
console and arenât sent and received between the console and CloudHub.

## Replace a Protected Property Value

After you set the property value, you canât retrieve it. However, you can replace the protected
property value with a new protected value:

1.  From Anypoint Platform, select **Runtime Manager** > **Applications**.
2.  Click the app name.
3.  In the navigation menu, click **Settings**.
4.  On the **Settings** page, click the **Properties** tab.
5.  Click **Table view**.
6.  Click **â¦â** > **Replace protected value**:

    ![Replace protected value option](_images/ch2-protected-props-replace.png)

7.  Enter a new value in the field.
8.  Click **Apply**, then **Apply Changes**.

## See Also

- [Secure Configuration Properties](../mule-runtime/latest/secure-configuration-properties)
- [Changing App Behavior with Properties](ch2-manage-props)
- [Deploying Apps to CloudHub 2.0](ch2-deploy)
---
